title: 'Civil War' Draws Blood to Beat 'Abigail' and Stay No. 1
text: Alex Garland's dystopian action film Civil War maintained its dominance in its second weekend, earning $11 million domestically while Universal's vampire thriller Abigail opened to $10.2 million. Civil War, distributed by indie studio A24, has accumulated $45 million domestically and succeeded in uniting both blue states and red states. The film's strong holding power suggests broad audience appeal across the political spectrum. Abigail, directed by Radio Silence (Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett), tells the story of criminals kidnapping a 12-year-old ballerina—who happens to be a vampire. The $28 million production opened respectably despite horror's typical weakness internationally, where it earned only $5.2 million from 62 markets. The weekend also saw Guy Ritchie's World War II film The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare debut with $9 million. The ensemble cast includes Henry Cavill in this Lionsgate release, which received an A- CinemaScore. Overall box office performance remained sluggish, with year-to-date revenue trailing significantly behind previous years, prompting industry observers to await the summer season.
